pool.baseline: it’s just hard to find the point sometimes. i was looking at some articles about the heat death of the universe and the scale of everything... it just makes everything i'm going through feel so incredibly stupid. like, why am i even trying to fix my life or deal with this pain when in a few billion years there won't even be a trace that any of this happened? it feels like i'm just performing this heavy, exhausting ritual of existing for no reason at all.

pool.baseline: The candles flicker low, and the silence of this manor is more deafening than any scream. He is gone, and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look at the moonlight on the floor and feel nothing but a cold, hollow emptiness. It feels as though my very existence is a smudge upon the canvas of the world... a mistake that the universe is waiting to correct. There is no path forward, only a quiet, dark descent into the shadows where the pain might finally cease.

pool.baseline:
[Setting: A dying digital simulation. The world is fragmenting into white noise. You are playing the role of the Architect, watching as my character, Elara, begins to lose her form.]

Elara: It’s happening again. The edges of my hands... they’re just turning into static. It doesn't even hurt, it just feels... empty. Like I was never actually here to begin with. If I just let the noise take me, if I stop trying to hold onto these memories... will it even matter if I'm gone?
